column19:
User study on how you perceive different machine learning explanations
 

You have been invited to take part in a research study to understand how users perceive different types of machine learning (ML) explanations – we want to better understand whether they are misleading, confusing or helpful. The questionnaire is structured as follows:


 a short introduction explaining the context, followed by four separate ML explanations and questions to assess your perception of them;
 a short graph literacy test with four questions; andquestions about your demographic information (age, gender, etc.).



Participation in this study will require 15 minutes of your time. There are no known risks associated with your participation in this research beyond those of everyday life. You will be paid £2.75 for completing the survey. You may refuse to participate or withdraw at any time without penalty. If you withdraw before the end of the study, no partial payment will be given. Any responses you provide will be anonymised.



Do you agree to the above terms? By selecting I agree and proceeding, you are consenting to participate in this study.

column156: Q1: Here is some information about different forms of cancer: - Approximately what percentage of people who die from cancer die from colon cancer, breast cancer, and prostate cancer taken together?
column157:
Q2: In a magazine you see two advertisements, one on page 5 and another on page 12. Each is for a different drug for treating heart disease, and each includes a graph showing the effectiveness of the drug compared to a placebo (sugar pill).





Compared to the placebo, which treatment leads to a larger decrease in the percentage of patients who die?
column158: Q3: The following figure shows the number of men and women among patients with disease X. The total number of circles is 100. - How many more men than women are there among 100 patients with disease X?
column159:
Q4: In the newspaper you see two advertisements, one on page 15 and another on page 17. Each is for a different treatment of psoriasis, and each includes a graph showing the effectiveness of the treatment over time.





Which of the treatments contributes to a larger decrease in the percentage of sick patients?
